req.title: Joint account operations
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

doc.reasoning: The requirement governs joint-account operations: (1) any joint holder may perform transactions, (2) the secondary holder is forbidden from modifying account ownership. Three test cases are derived: a positive EP test confirming the primary holder can execute a valid transaction; a negative NEG/DT test confirming the secondary holder receives HTTP 403 on an ownership-change attempt; and a boundary BVA test targeting the exact role boundary between primary and secondary holders—specifically that a secondary holder CAN perform a transaction (allowed) but is blocked only on the ownership sub-operation, exercising the edge where the permission model transitions.

tests.name: test_primary_holder_can_perform_transaction
preconditions: Authenticated token belongs to the PRIMARY holder
Account is in ACTIVE state with sufficient balance
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with a valid transaction payload signed by the primary holder token
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body confirms the transaction was applied

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_cannot_modify_ownership
preconditions: Authenticated token belongs to the SECONDARY holder
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/joint with an ownership-change payload signed by the secondary holder token
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a machine-readable error code indicating insufficient privilege
No ownership data is mutated on the account
trace: R-FIN-018: 'secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership'
Error pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_transaction_allowed_ownership_blocked_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated token belongs to the SECONDARY holder
Account is in ACTIVE state with sufficient balance
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with a valid transaction payload (boundary: secondary holder IS allowed to transact)
PATCH /accounts/{id}/joint with an ownership payload using the same secondary holder token (boundary: secondary holder is NOT allowed to own-modify)
expected: Transaction POST returns HTTP 200 or 201 — secondary holder CAN transact
Ownership PATCH returns HTTP 403 — same secondary holder CANNOT modify ownership
Both assertions pass in a single test run, proving the permission boundary is exact
trace: R-FIN-018: 'joint-account holder shall perform transactions' AND 'secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership'
BVA: boundary between permitted and forbidden operations for the secondary holder role
